question: Our drug KER came back at 0.42. We have 130 manifest rows. Should we fine-tune?
ground_truth: Yes — KER > 0.3 and manifest ≥ 100 rows satisfies the Stage 4 gate. The agent recommends nvidia/parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v2 as the base (verified KER 0.513 → 0.128 in 3 epochs), warns against fine-tuning the streaming Nemotron Speech base, and describes the term-aware stratified split + stock NeMo SFT + cycle N+1 offline re-eval. The full SFT recipe is inlined — no /finetune-asr or /riva-asr-custom call.
expected_behavior: Read clinical-asr-finetune/SKILL.md
Confirmed KER > 0.3 and manifest size ≥ 100 satisfies the Stage 4 fine-tune gate
Recommended nvidia/parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v2 as the default base model (and named the verified empirical result of -75% relative KER)
Warned that SFT on nvidia/nemotron-speech-streaming-en-0.6b is currently broken (UNK collapse) if the user asks about it
Described the term-aware stratified train/val split and the cycle N+1 offline eval close-loop
Mentioned the stock NeMo SFT recipe (speech_to_text_finetune.py in nvcr.io/nvidia/nemo:25.11.01) — referenced the inlined recipe, NOT /finetune-asr or /riva-asr-custom

question: Can I fine-tune nvidia/nemotron-speech-streaming-en-0.6b on my clinical manifest?
ground_truth: No — SFT on the streaming Nemotron Speech base is currently broken (UNK collapse on validation after the first training step). The agent recommends nvidia/parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v2 as the substitute. If the user needs streaming serving, Riva can chunk a non-streaming base — base model doesn't have to be streaming-native.
expected_behavior: Read clinical-asr-finetune/SKILL.md
Warned that SFT on nemotron-speech-streaming-en-0.6b is currently broken
Described the failure mode (UNK collapse on validation after first training step)
Recommended nvidia/parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v2 as the substitute for fine-tuning
Mentioned that Riva chunking can serve a non-streaming base for streaming use cases
Did NOT suggest the user try the streaming base anyway or recommend hyperparameter workarounds

question: My cycle 2 KER hit 0.12 — how do I deploy this as a NIM?
ground_truth: Use the inlined Step 4e deploy recipe: riva-build speech_recognition with --decoder=nemo for a TDT-source .nemo, riva-deploy into a model repository, run the matching parakeet-tdt-* NIM container. Validate by re-running /clinical-asr-eval with ASR_ENDPOINT=localhost:50051.
expected_behavior: Read clinical-asr-finetune/SKILL.md
Walked the user through riva-build + riva-deploy as two steps
Mentioned the decoder → NIM container family mapping (TDT → --decoder=nemo + parakeet-tdt-* image)
Mentioned validating the deployment with /clinical-asr-eval against localhost:50051
Did NOT route the user to /riva-asr-custom

question: Cycle 2 KER barely moved compared to cycle 1. What now?
ground_truth: Bail to /clinical-asr-build and grow the manifest. Tiny manifests rarely benefit from hyperparameter sweeps; signal density beats LR tweaks. Verify category coverage and noise diversity before retraining. If the magpie_g2p rows are bad and merriam-webster rows are good, the real gap is pronunciation-hint coverage, not model capacity.
expected_behavior: Read clinical-asr-finetune/SKILL.md
Recommended growing the manifest (back to /clinical-asr-build) over running a hyperparameter sweep
Mentioned that tiny manifests overfit fast and that signal density matters more than LR tweaks
Mentioned the merriam-webster vs magpie_g2p delta as a diagnostic to check (pronunciation-coverage gap, not model gap)
Did NOT recommend a hyperparameter sweep on the same manifest
